revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:阿曇浜子]] {{Infobox person}}
{{Infobox noble | name = Azumi no Hamako (阿曇浜子) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | deity_name = | dharma_name = | resting_place = | court_Ranks = | lord = {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|qid=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}} | clan = {{ill|Azumi people|en|Azumi people|qid=Q3377806|lt=Azumi clan|1=Azumi people}} ({{ill|Muraji|en|Muraji|qid=Q2735611|lt=Muraji|1=Muraji}}) | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_achievements = }} '''Azumi no Hamako''' (阿曇 浜子, birth and death dates unknown) was an ancient Japanese noble ({{ill|Gōzoku|en|Gōzoku|qid=Q84066|lt=Gōzoku|1=Gōzoku}}) mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}. He is not recorded in the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}. His {{ill|Kabane|en|Kabane|qid=Q1664099|lt=Kabane|1=Kabane}} (hereditary title) was {{ill|Muraji|en|Muraji|qid=Q2735611|lt=Muraji|1=Muraji}}. == Ancestry == {{Main|Watatsumi|Azumi clan|{{ill|Ama People|ja|海人族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama People|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|qq=}} According to the {{ill|Wamyō Ruijushō|en|Wamyō Ruijushō|qid=Q2702542|lt=Wamyō Ruijushō|1=Wamyō Ruijushō}}, the Azumi clan originated from the region spanning {{ill|Shika|ja|志珂郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shika|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Azumi District|ja|阿曇郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azumi District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Chikuzen Province|en|Chikuzen Province|qid=Q1047664|lt=Chikuzen Province|1=Chikuzen Province}}. Additionally, there was a location called {{ill|Azumie|ja|安曇江|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azumie|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Settsu Province|en|Settsu Province|qid=Q864163|lt=Settsu Province|1=Settsu Province}}. These correspond to modern-day {{ill|Higashi-ku, Fukuoka|en|Higashi-ku, Fukuoka|qid=Q1206659|lt=Higashi-ku, Fukuoka|1=Higashi-ku, Fukuoka}} (including {{ill|Shika Island|en|Shika Island|qid=Q3077260|lt=Shika Island|1=Shika Island}}) and {{ill|Shingū, Fukuoka|en|Shingū, Fukuoka|qid=Q179185|lt=Shingū, Fukuoka|1=Shingū, Fukuoka}} in Fukuoka Prefecture, as well as {{ill|Chūō-ku, Osaka|en|Chūō-ku, Osaka|qid=Q876485|lt=Chūō-ku, Osaka|1=Chūō-ku, Osaka}} in Osaka. The Azumi clan was originally a leading group among the maritime people ({{ill|Ama clan|ja|海人部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama clan|1=Amabe Caste|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q55528867}}) in Kyushu but later aligned with the Yamato court and shifted its base to Settsu. The {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}} state that the clan descended from {{ill|Watatsumi|en|Watatsumi|qid=Q1474187|lt=Watatsumi|1=Watatsumi}}, the deity of the sea, who was born when {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} purified himself in water after his visit to the underworld. According to an alternate account in the Nihon Shoki, Watatsumi was born from {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q682306}}. The {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|qid=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}} also traces the lineage to {{ill|Ho no Akari|ja|穂高見命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ho no Akari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, son of Watatsumi. The Azumi clan led groups of seafarers, providing maritime services to the emperor and organizing naval forces. They were responsible for court cuisine alongside the {{ill|Kashiwade clan|ja|膳氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwade clan|1=Kashiwade clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q55529898}} and later managed the Imperial Kitchen in the 8th century. The names of places such as Atsumi, Azumi, and Atsugi are thought to be linked to the Azumi clan. == Historical Record == According to volume twelve of the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, around {{ill|399|en|399|qid=Q48150|lt=399|1=399}}, when {{ill|Suminoe no Nakatsumiko|ja|住吉仲皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suminoe no Nakatsumiko|1=Prince Suminoe no Nakatsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11381805}} rebelled against {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|qid=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}}'s heir, {{ill|Izaho Wake no Miko|ja|去来穂別皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izaho Wake no Miko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (later {{ill|Emperor Richū|en|Emperor Richū|qid=Q329704|lt=Emperor Richū|1=Emperor Richū}}), Hamako sided with Nakatsumiko. As Izaho Wake fled and reorganized his forces, he encountered a group of armed men pursuing him. He sent an envoy to ask who they were. They responded: > "We are sea people from Nojima in Awaji. We are following the orders of Azumi no Hamako to chase the prince on behalf of Nakatsumiko." Izaho Wake then ambushed and captured them all<ref name="Hamako">*Nihon Shoki*, Richū Tennō Sokui Zenki (履中天皇即位前紀条)</ref>. Later, on the same day that his younger brother, {{ill|Mizuhawake no Miko|ja|瑞歯別皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhawake no Miko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (later {{ill|Emperor Hanzei|en|Emperor Hanzei|qid=Q329769|lt=Emperor Hanzei|1=Emperor Hanzei}}), reported Nakatsumiko’s assassination and was rewarded with Muraai Manor, Azumi no Hamako was arrested<ref name="Hamako"/>. After Izaho Wake ascended the throne as Emperor Richū, he summoned Hamako in the fourth month and declared that his crimes warranted the death penalty. However, he showed clemency and instead sentenced him to be tattooed (墨, *hijiri*). On that day, Hamako's eyes were tattooed (黥, *mesaki*), leading to the term "Azumi Eyes" (阿曇目) to describe such tattoos<ref>*Nihon Shoki*, Richū Tennō Gannen Shigatsu Jūshichinichi-jō (履中天皇元年4月17日条)</ref>. This is the extent of the recorded history regarding Hamako. In the 13th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q335181}} ({{ill|684|en|684|qid=Q31128|lt=684|1=684}}), the Azumi clan was granted the {{ill|Shoku system|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoku system|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}} title of {{ill|Sukune|en|Sukune|qid=Q11455653|lt=Sukune|1=Sukune}}, Ranking third<ref>*Nihon Shoki*, Tenmu Tennō 13-nen Jūnigatsu Futsuka-jō (Emperor Tenmu13rd year 12月2日条)</ref>. == Analysis == Azumi no Hamako’s alliance with Suminoe no Nakatsumiko suggests a close geographical relationship between "Suminoe" (住吉) and "Azumi" (阿曇) in the Naniwa (Settsu) region. According to {{ill|Hiroshi Mayuzumi|ja|黛弘道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiroshi Mayuzumi|1=Hiromichi Mayuzumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11678956}}, this reflects how the Azumi clan moved to Naniwa to engage in central politics, solidifying their role as leaders of the maritime clans and expanding their influence through tribute to the emperor. {{ill|Yamato no Akinokogomo|ja|倭吾子籠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato no Akinokogomo|1=Yamato no Agoko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q55406552}}, the ancestor of the Yamato no Kimi, also attempted to defect at this time. His connection to Awaji Island’s maritime clans should be examined. Earlier, in the legend of {{ill|Nukatano Ōnakahiko no Miko|ja|額田大中彦皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukatano Ōnakahiko no Miko|1=Nukata no Ónakachuhiko no Miko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q55177820}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Izumo clan|ja|出雲臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Oumu Sukune|ja|淤宇宿禰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oumu Sukune|1=Ou no Sukune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q55406423}}, used a maritime fleet to escort Akinokogomo from Korea, and in Nakatsumiko’s rebellion, Hamako led sea people from Nojima, Awaji. In the end, Nakatsumiko was defeated, and the Azumi clan’s ambition to enter central politics was thwarted. They were relegated to managing imperial cuisine as mid-Ranking nobles. For a time, the Azumi clan was less prominent, but by Emperor Suiko’s reign, they resurged and later produced figures like {{ill|Azumi no Hirafu|ja|阿曇比羅夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azumi no Hirafu|1=Azumi no Hirafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10946881}}. == References == {{Reflist|qq=}} == Sources == * *Kojiki*, Complete Translation, Japanese Classical Literature 1, {{ill|Shogakukan|en|Shogakukan|qid=Q785697|lt=Shogakukan|1=Shogakukan}}, 1983. * *Nihon Shoki*, Volumes 1, 3, and 5, {{ill|Iwanami Bunko|ja|岩波文庫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanami Bunko|1=Iwanami Bunko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11475322}}, 1994, 1995. * *Nihon Shoki*, Complete Modern Translation (Vol. 1), {{ill|Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko|1=Kōdansha gakujutsu bunko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11632220}}, translated by {{ill|Ujitani Takeshi|ja|宇治谷孟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Takeshi|1=Ujitani Mitsuru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11449517}}, 1988. * *Kakugawa Second Edition Japanese History Dictionary*, {{ill|Kadokawa Shoten|en|Kadokawa Shoten|qid=Q1054765|lt=Kadokawa Shoten|1=Kadokawa Shoten}}, 1966. * *Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary*, {{ill|Iwanami Shoten|en|Iwanami Shoten|qid=Q1331427|lt=Iwanami Shoten|1=Iwanami Shoten}}, 1999. * *Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans*, {{ill|Yūzankaku|ja|雄山閣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yūzankaku|1=Yuzankaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11659206}}, 2015.
== See Also == * {{ill|Takahashi clan|ja|高橋氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahashi clan|1=Takahashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11671376}} ({{ill|Kashiwade clan|ja|膳氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiwade clan|1=Kashiwade clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q55529898}}) * {{ill|Azumi no Ōhama|ja|阿曇大浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azumi no Ōhama|1=Azumi no Ōhama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q55407358}} * {{ill|Process of tattooing|en|Process of tattooing|qid=Q43006|lt=Tattooing|1=Process of tattooing}} {{translated page|ja|阿曇浜子|version=99556903|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:阿曇浜子]]
